artifacts.content:
Hi Ba,

Mum said you were asking how everything is going, so I thought I’d write properly instead of just sending memes on Zalo.

School’s been pretty full-on. Since that panic episode during the Year 9 PE beep test at Ryde Secondary in late May, Ms Rebecca Collins (the school counsellor) keeps checking in. It was so embarrassing, I felt like I couldn’t breathe and my chest was tight, and everyone thought it was just my asthma. Our GP at Ryde Medical Centre ended up sending me to the paediatric respiratory clinic at Westmead Children’s Hospital to see if the exercise‑induced asthma is mixed up with anxiety.

I’ve had two 45‑minute appointments there so far. The doctor there wants me to start this breathing therapy thing, but Mum and Dad are still talking about it. They don’t want me missing too much class or basketball training, especially with the regional comp coming up in March.

On top of that, my asthma has been annoying since the Year 8 camp at Narrabeen last September. I forgot my Ventolin for the night hike, started wheezing, and Mr James O’Connor had to write an incident report. Then the deputy principal, Mrs Karen Li, emailed Mum saying if I forget my inhaler again for a camp they might not let me go on overnighters. Now Mum keeps a spare inhaler in the kitchen drawer at 42 Willowbank Street and double‑checks my bag.

Work at the Ryde Community Sports Centre café is still good. Saturday mornings are busy with kids’ games, but I like earning my own money. I’m still saving for a gaming laptop, unless I cave and buy new basketball shoes first.

Say hi to Ong Noi and Ba Noi for me. I’ll try to jump on the Sunday video call after homework.

Love,
Thomas

metadata.subject: Update about school, asthma stuff and everything else

artifacts.content:
Notes for myself – health / sport / church stuff

1. Concussion + activity limits
- Remember what Dr. Patel at Ryde Medical Centre said after the indoor soccer concussion at Ryde Community Sports Centre on 12 August 2023.
- No contact sports or heavy lifting for about 6 months after that – I’m basically past that now, but I still get mild headaches and light sensitivity if I don’t sleep enough.
- Mum (Lan) keeps that note on the fridge about not doing more than 2–3 hours of intense activity in one day at youth or church events. Probably smart with all the running games and chair‑moving.

2. Asthma + camps
- Exercise‑induced asthma diagnosed ages ago. ALWAYS pack blue Ventolin inhaler.
- Don’t repeat the Year 8 camp at Narrabeen (Sept 2023) where I forgot it for the night hike, wheezed, and Mr James O’Connor had to file an incident report.
- Mrs Karen Li warned Mum they might not approve me for future overnight camps if I forget meds again.
- Spare inhaler lives in the kitchen drawer at 42 Willowbank Street – grab it for camps/youth.

3. Youth nights / dizziness
- Since Ba Ngoai (Huong Tran) got type 2 diabetes at Eastwood Medical Practice in Oct 2022, Mum worries when I look pale.
- After my flu shot in April 2024, GP ordered fasting bloods at Douglass Hanly Moir Pathology in Top Ryde – results were normal.
- Still: if I feel dizzy or shaky after 30–40 minutes of games or moving chairs, SIT DOWN, drink water, snack, tell a leader/Mum.

4. General
- Keep antihistamines + Opal card in inner pocket.
- Sleep > late‑night gaming, especially before PE or basketball.

Dear Ms Taylor,

I hope you’re well. Mum asked me to forward some information about my allergy and asthma so that it’s clear for upcoming excursions and sports days.

On 3 February 2024 I accidentally ate cashew in a noodle dish at Saigon Lane in Eastwood when I was out with friends. I broke out in hives around my mouth and felt really nauseous. Mum (Lan) had to leave her Saturday shift at Ryde Community Pharmacy about 2 hours early to take me to Ryde Hospital urgent care. The doctor there wrote a note explaining that I need to avoid all mixed‑nut snacks at group events and that supervising adults should have my parents’ mobile numbers and be ready to call if I vomit or my lips swell, even if I say I’m fine (I get embarrassed in front of younger kids).

I also have mild exercise‑induced asthma. During the Year 8 camp at Narrabeen in September 2023, I had a wheezing episode after the night hike because I’d forgotten to pack my Ventolin inhaler. Mr James O’Connor completed an incident report and the deputy principal, Mrs Karen Li, emailed Mum a warning that if I forget my medication again for a school camp, I might not be approved for overnight activities. Since then, Mum keeps a spare inhaler in the kitchen drawer at home (42 Willowbank Street) and double‑checks that I’ve packed one for any excursion.

Day to day, my asthma is well controlled. I usually just take a puff before PE or basketball if it’s going to be intense. I’m happy to remind teachers myself, but Mum thought it would help if you knew the background in case there are shared snack tables or longer hikes on excursions.

Thank you for your help with this.

Kind regards,
Thomas Nguyen (9B)

metadata.subject: Allergy and asthma information for excursions

artifacts.content:
Event: Westmead Children’s Hospital – paediatric respiratory clinic
Date: Friday 26 July 2024
Time: 3:15 pm – 4:45 pm (allow 45‑minute appointment + check‑in)
Location: Westmead Children’s Hospital, paediatric respiratory clinic
Attendees: Me, Mum (Lan) – Dad if he can get off work from the network job.

Notes:
- This is my 3rd visit after the panic episode during the Year 9 PE beep test at Ryde Secondary College in late May 2024.
- GP at Ryde Medical Centre referred me to check if my exercise‑induced asthma is linked with anxiety.
- They already did two 45‑minute appointments with breathing tests and questions.

Things to ask:
1. Breathing therapy
- Doctor suggested starting breathing therapy; Mum and Dad still deciding.
- Ask how many sessions, how often, and if it clashes with Tuesday/Thursday basketball training at Ryde Community Sports Centre.
- Ask if it’ll help with both actual asthma and panic feelings.

2. Sport limits
- Mention old concussion from indoor soccer at Ryde Community Sports Centre on 12 August 2023 (Dr. Patel said no contact sports/heavy lifting for ~6 months back then; mostly ok now but still get headaches if I don’t sleep).
- Ask if it’s safe to push harder for the regional basketball comp in late March.

3. Practical stuff
- Remind Mum we need to bring Ventolin, list of any recent wheezing episodes (none since Narrabeen camp Sept 2023), and Medicare card.
- Leave school at 2:15 pm – sign out at office.
- Pack headphones + maths homework for the car ride.

Hi Di Hoa,

Mum said you wanted to know how Ba Ngoai and I are doing, so I thought I’d write.

Ba Ngoai is mostly okay, just still dealing with the type 2 diabetes she was diagnosed with at Eastwood Medical Practice back in October 2022. Sometimes she forgets to eat regularly, so when we visit her unit in Eastwood on Fridays, Mum makes sure she has proper food in the fridge. I help carry the grocery bags up the stairs.

Because of Ba Ngoai’s diabetes, Mum got a bit worried about me too. When I went for my flu shot in April 2024, she asked our GP to check if my occasional dizziness at youth nights might be blood sugar stuff. He sent me for a fasting blood test at Douglass Hanly Moir Pathology in Top Ryde. The results were normal, which was a relief, but he told Mum to keep an eye on me if I look pale or shaky when I’m helping with games or moving chairs for more than 30–40 minutes.

On top of that, I’m still dealing with breathing things. You probably heard from Mum that during a Year 9 PE beep test at Ryde Secondary in late May I had a bit of a panic episode and couldn’t catch my breath properly. The school counsellor, Ms Rebecca Collins, thought it might be tied to my exercise‑induced asthma, so our GP referred me to the paediatric respiratory clinic at Westmead Children’s Hospital. I’ve had two 45‑minute appointments there so far. They’re talking about starting me on some breathing therapy, but Mum and Dad haven’t decided yet.

Apart from all the health stuff, life is normal – school, basketball training at Ryde Community Sports Centre, and my weekend café job there. I’m still saving for a gaming laptop and trying not to spend everything on snacks at Top Ryde.

Love,
Thomas

metadata.subject: Update on Ba Ngoai and my health

artifacts.content:
Sunday planning – school, sport, health

1. Week overview
- Mon: normal classes, STEM club after school.
- Tue: Basketball training at Ryde Community Sports Centre (guard drills). Take Ventolin puff before warm‑up.
- Wed: English group assignment meeting at library.
- Thu: Training again – don’t overdo it, remember Dr. Patel’s warning after the 12 Aug 2023 concussion from indoor soccer at Ryde Community Sports Centre (no heavy lifting/contact for 6 months back then; still get mild headaches + light sensitivity if I stay up too late).
- Fri: Family visit to Ba Ngoai in Eastwood + groceries.
- Sat: Café shift 8:30–12 at Ryde Community Sports Centre café.
- Sun: Youth group + maybe park hoops.

2. Health reminders
- Asthma: Exercise‑induced. ALWAYS pack Ventolin. Don’t repeat Narrabeen Sept 2023 night hike drama (forgot inhaler → wheezing → Mr James O’Connor incident report → email from Mrs Karen Li threatening future camp approvals). Spare inhaler = kitchen drawer at 42 Willowbank Street.
- Allergy: Cashew reaction at Saigon Lane Eastwood on 3 Feb 2024 → hives + nausea → Mum left Ryde Community Pharmacy 2 hrs early → Ryde Hospital urgent care. Avoid mixed‑nut snacks at youth/parties. Keep non‑drowsy antihistamine strip in backpack inner pocket with Opal card.
- Dizziness: Fasting bloods at Douglass Hanly Moir Top Ryde after flu shot in April 2024 were normal, but if I feel pale/shaky after 30–40 mins of games or chair‑moving at youth, SIT, water, snack, tell leader.
- Anxiety/breathing: Late May 2024 beep test panic at Ryde Secondary → referral to Westmead Children’s Hospital paediatric respiratory clinic. Two 45‑min appointments done; possible breathing therapy coming. Try box‑breathing before PE.

3. To‑dos
- Charge phone + pack headphones for Westmead trip.
- Ask coach about minutes limit at regional comp.
- Sleep by 10:30 on school nights – no "one more game" after that.

Notes to self:

- Date Of Birth: 2010-08-17
- Phone Number: (041) 234-5678
- Parent Guardian Contact Number: (042) 987-6543
